If Jehoiakim had served the Lord, he would not have been a servant to Nebuchadnezzar. If he had been content with his servitude and true to his word, his condition would have been no worse; but by rebelling against Babylon, he plunged himself into more trouble. See what need nations have to lament the sins of their fathers, lest they suffer for them. Threatenings will be fulfilled as certainly as promises, if a sinner's repentance does not prevent it.

Jehoiachin reigned only three months, yet long enough to show that he justly suffered for his fathers’ sins, because he followed in their steps. His uncle was entrusted with the government. This Zedekiah was the last of the kings of Judah. Though the judgments of God upon the three kings before him might have warned him, he did what was evil, like them. When those entrusted with the counsel of a nation act unwisely and against their true interest, we ought to notice the displeasure of God in it. It is because of the sins of a people that God hides from them things that belong to public peace. And in fulfilling the secret purposes of his justice, the Lord needs only leave men to the blindness of their own minds or to the lusts of their own hearts. The gradual approach of Divine judgments affords sinners space for repentance and believers leisure to prepare for meeting the calamity, while it shows the obstinacy of those who will not forsake their sins.
